student-info-system.overview:
Console application to collect and display student details.

Features to build:
• Name
• Age
• Roll Number
• Branch
• College
• Display Details

Requirements:
• Use Scanner for input
• Store data in variables
• Display formatted output
student-info-system.explanation: Scanner reads each field from the keyboard. String fields use nextLine(); integers use nextInt() followed by nextLine() to consume the leftover newline. Variables store name, age, roll, branch, and college. printf formats a clean summary table. Always call sc.close() when done.
student-info-system.sampleInput:
Student name: Riya Sharma
Age: 19
Roll number: 101
Branch: CSE
College: Prathyu Academy
student-info-system.sampleOutput:
--- Student Details ---
Name    : Riya Sharma
Age     : 19
Roll No : 101
Branch  : CSE
College : Prathyu Academy

simple-calculator.overview:
Menu-driven calculator with basic arithmetic operations.

Features to build:
• Addition
• Subtraction
• Multiplication
• Division
• Modulus

Requirements:
• Handle division by zero
• Use switch-case or if-else for operations
simple-calculator.explanation: A menu-driven calculator using switch on the user's choice. Each case performs one operation (+, -, *, /, %). Division checks for zero before dividing. Scanner reads choice and two numbers as doubles so division gives decimal results.
simple-calculator.sampleInput:
1. Addition
2. Subtraction
3. Multiplication
4. Division
5. Modulus
Choice: 1
First number: 10
Second number: 4

bmi-calculator.overview:
Calculate Body Mass Index from height and weight input.

Features to build:
• Height (Input)
• Weight (Input)
• BMI (Output)
• Category (Output)

Requirements:
• Validate input ranges
• Display BMI category based on result
bmi-calculator.explanation: BMI = weight / (height × height). Input is validated so height and weight must be positive. if-else if chain maps BMI ranges to categories (Underweight, Normal, Overweight, Obese) using standard WHO thresholds.

student-grade-calculator.overview:
Calculate total, average, and percentage from 5 subject marks.

Features to build:
• 5 Subjects (Input)
• Total (Output)
• Average (Output)
• Percentage (Output)

Requirements:
• Accept marks for 5 subjects
• Calculate and display results
student-grade-calculator.explanation: A for loop reads marks for 5 subjects into an array and accumulates total. Average = total / 5.0 (note the 5.0 double literal so division is not integer). Percentage equals average when each subject is out of 100.
student-grade-calculator.sampleInput:
Subject 1 marks: 85
Subject 2 marks: 90
Subject 3 marks: 78
Subject 4 marks: 92
Subject 5 marks: 88

electricity-bill-calculator.overview:
Calculate electricity bill amount based on units consumed.

Features to build:
• Units (Input)
• Bill Amount (Output)

Requirements:
• Read units consumed
• Apply tariff/slab logic and display bill amount
electricity-bill-calculator.explanation: Slab-based billing: first 100 units at Rs. 3.5/unit, next 100 at Rs. 4.5, above 200 at Rs. 6.0. if-else if picks the correct tariff. Invalid negative units are rejected early with return.
